novel_features.name: Filing Anomaly Audit
novel_features.description: Flag amendments, terminations, duplicate-risk filings, client/registrant conflicts, and missing source URLs.
novel_features.rationale: Requires local joins across filings, registrants, clients, lobbyists, and constants.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs reproducible watchdog flags instead of raw filing rows.

novel_features.name: Entity Resolve Dossier
novel_features.description: Rank matching registrants, clients, and lobbyists with official IDs, counts, last activity, and source URLs.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a multi-resource local FTS index and normalized entity relationships.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before citing or joining an ambiguous company, firm, or lobbyist name.

novel_features.name: Lobbying Spend Timeline
novel_features.description: Aggregate disclosed lobbying income and expenses by client, registrant, issue, year, and quarter.
novel_features.rationale: Requires longitudinal local aggregation over nested filing detail and issue data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs analysis-ready spend totals rather than individual filings.

novel_features.name: Relationship Graph Export
novel_features.description: Export client-registrant-lobbyist-issue-government entity edges for graph analysis.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining synced filing detail into reusable relationship edges.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs a relationship graph for downstream NetworkX, Gephi, or SQL work.

novel_features.name: Contribution Counterparty Totals
novel_features.description: Aggregate LD-203 contribution items by contributor, recipient, payee, item type, year, and report.
novel_features.rationale: Requires flattening and summing nested contribution item arrays across local contribution reports.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs contribution totals without writing a custom flattener.

novel_features.name: Quarterly Activity Report
novel_features.description: Produce quarter stats for filings, amendments, terminations, top issues, top entities, spend, and LD-203 totals.
novel_features.rationale: Requires combining filings, contributions, constants, source URLs, and local stale-data checks.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this after quarterly deadlines when an agent needs a compact, source-linked snapshot.

novel_features.name: Covered Positions Map
novel_features.description: List lobbyists' covered government positions connected to clients, registrants, and filing periods.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining lobbyist covered-position fields to filing relationships and source records.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs exact official covered-position evidence tied to active lobbying records.

narrative.headline: Lobbying disclosure records with local sync, entity resolution, and evidence-grade exports.
narrative.value_prop: Search the official Lobbying Disclosure Act API, sync public filings into SQLite, and turn nested lobbying records into source-linked audits. The CLI keeps anonymous reads safe by default, supports optional registered API keys, and adds commands for entity resolution, anomaly checks, spend timelines, contribution totals, and graph exports.
narrative.auth_narrative: LDA.gov works anonymously at a lower rate limit. For higher throughput, register for an API key at https://lda.gov/api/register/ and set LDA_API_KEY to the key value; the CLI sends it as Authorization: Token <key>. Legacy scripts may use SENATE_LDA_API_KEY or USSLDA_KEY, but new usage should prefer LDA_API_KEY.

quickstart.comment: Check CLI configuration without making a network request.
Seed a bounded local mirror for analysis without exhausting anonymous quota.
Search the synced filing corpus locally.
Resolve an ambiguous name across registrants, clients, and lobbyists.

troubleshoots.fix: Wait for the Retry-After window, lower --max-pages, or use a registered key with LDA_API_KEY.

troubleshoots.symptom: Pagination fails after page 1 for filings or contributions
troubleshoots.fix: Add a filter such as --filing-year 2024 or sync with --resource-param filings:filing_year=2024.
Use the default lda.gov base URL; lda.senate.gov is deprecated and sunsets in 2026.

troubleshoots.symptom: Audit command returns an empty JSON array
troubleshoots.fix: Run sync first with the resources named by the command and pass the same --db path.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you need official LDA.gov records, local analysis, repeatable watchdog checks, or agent-friendly evidence exports. It is strongest for lobbying filings, contribution reports, registrant/client/lobbyist lookup, source citation, and longitudinal analysis across synced data.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for FEC campaign-finance data; use a dedicated FEC source.
Do not use this CLI for legal advice about LDA filing obligations.
Do not use this CLI for live bill status or congressional vote data; use Congress.gov or another legislative API.
Do not use this CLI to infer missed filing obligations without corroborating records.

recipes.title: Resolve a company before joining records
recipes.explanation: Ranks official registrant, client, and lobbyist matches so an agent can pick the right ID.

recipes.title: Build a source-linked quarter snapshot
recipes.explanation: Narrows a potentially large report to the fields an agent needs in context.

recipes.title: Audit filings for cleanup flags
recipes.explanation: Flags amendments, terminations, duplicate risks, and client/registrant conflicts.

recipes.title: Export a lobbying relationship graph
recipes.explanation: Creates edge rows connecting clients, registrants, lobbyists, issues, and government entities.

recipes.title: Summarize LD-203 contribution counterparties
recipes.explanation: Flattens contribution items and aggregates them by counterparty and item type.
